items.title: Earth Day Command Connection
items.description: This video highlights Earth Day on Spangdahlem Air Base, Germany, April 24, 2026. U.S. Air Force Col. Rob Kochan, 52nd Munitions Maintenance Group commander, stopped by the studio to share what he’s learned environmentally from managing the 52nd MMG's specialized equipment and sites, and to provide a few tips for people getting rid of belongings during the upcoming PCS season. (U.S. Air Force video by Airman 1st Class Ryan Bratton)

items.title: Constant readiness: USFJ hones typhoon response
items.description: In the world's most active typhoon region, readiness is a constant state. For U.S. Forces Japan, sharpening that readiness is the mission of Typhoon Ready, an annual command post exercise designed to test and refine the command’s response to severe weather threats. Long before any real storm makes landfall, personnel rehearse the critical actions needed to protect lives and vital military assets.

items.title: Kadena KC-135 Stratotanker certifies Kunsan Airmen on hot pit procedures
items.description: A KC-135 Stratotanker from Kadena Air Base conducted hot pit refueling at Kunsan Air Base, Republic of Korea, April 22, 2026. Members of the 8th Logistics Readiness Squadron fuels management flight used the refueling operation to certify Airmen on heavy aircraft refueling procedures, strengthening Kunsan’s ability to support a wider range of aircraft across the Wolf Pack mission. (U.S. Air Force video by Senior Airman Mason Hargrove)

items.title: Lethal Kinetic Strike, April 26, 2026
items.description: On April 26, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command, Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations. Intelligence confirmed the vessel was transiting along known narco-trafficking routes in the Eastern Pacific and was engaged in narco-trafficking operations. Three male narco-terrorists were killed during this action. No U.S. military forces were harmed.

items.title: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (CVN 69) Completes PIA Early at NNSY
items.description:
USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (CVN 69) has completed sea trials, marking the successful early completion of its Planned Incremental Availability (PIA) at Norfolk Naval Shipyard (NNSY). Check out this timelapse of Mighty IKE's departure! You can read the full story here: https://www.dvidshub.net/news/563538/uss-dwight-d-eisenhower-completes-planned-incremental-availability-norfolk-naval-shipyard

Video by Dave Pastoriza, C1571 Videographer

items.title: Flintlock’s 91st Civil Affairs Battalion Invests in the future of Côte d’Ivoire
items.description: During Exercise Flintlock 26, U.S. Soldiers from the 91st Civil Affairs Battalion and Ivorian civil-military cooperation soldiers visited a primary school in Ouffoué-Diékro, Côte d'Ivoire, to foster community trust and support youth education. The ceremony honored the donation of school supplies to over 600 children, underscoring the commitment to the region's long-term development beyond traditional military operations. items.title: USNH Rota Builds Readiness, Strengthens Spanish Partnership During Emergency Drill
items.description: ROTA, Spain (February 12, 2026) — A routine day quickly turned into a high-stakes scenario at U.S. Naval Hospital Rota as staff responded to a simulated neonatal emergency, working side by side with Spain’s 061 public ambulance service, which provides emergency medical response and transport.

(U.S. Navy photo by Mass Communication Specialist 1st Class John Bellino)

items.title: Lethal Kinetic Strike, April 24, 2026
items.description: On April 24, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command, Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations. Intelligence confirmed the vessel was transiting along known narco-trafficking routes in the Eastern Pacific and was engaged in narco-trafficking operations. Two male narco-terrorists were killed during this action. No U.S. military forces were harmed.

items.title: Iron sharpens iron
items.description: An unusually warm day in central Minnesota set the tone for the 2026 Minnesota Best Warrior Competition. The twist came within hours, sunshine turned to rain, the ground turned to mud before snowflakes fell from the sky. Over three days, Camp Ripley Training Center saw highs drop from 70 degrees to just 30, in time for one of the state’s most demanding competitions.

items.title: Connecticut National Guard hosts first Defense Cyber Operations Symposium for Cyber Yankee 2026
items.description: From April 10–12, 2026, the Connecticut National Guard hosted its first Defensive Cyber Operations (DCO) Symposium at Camp Nett, bringing together over 100 cyber soldiers from across New England to enhance readiness ahead of the upcoming Cyber Yankee exercise. The event marked the first time Connecticut has hosted a symposium of this kind, featuring participation from the 126th Cyber Protection Battalion and strengthening collaboration across New England cyber forces.

items.title: Commander of Unmanned Surface Vessel Division 12 concludes early command tour
items.description: PORT HUENEME, Calif. (Feb. 13, 2026) — Lt. Cmdr. Chris Rodman relinquished command of Unmanned Surface Vessel Division (USVDIV) 12 after successfully completing his early command tour. LCDR Rodman served as the first commanding officer of USVDIV 12, assigned to Unmanned Surface Vessel Squadron (USVRON) 1, where he played a pivotal role in introducing and developing Medium Unmanned Surface Vessels (mUSVs) for integration into the Surface Fleet.

items.title: NMCCL workflow modernization positively enhances provider/patient encounters
items.description: Modernization efforts at Naval Medical Center Camp Lejeune (NMCCL) are improving the workday for providers and enhancing patient interactions. Following the adoption of new administrative documentation workflows, providers are spending an average of 1.2 hours less per day on charting, contributing to a 26% reduction in after-hours work. At the heart of this transformation is ambient listening technology.

items.title: Recovery Update 3: Coast Guard crews make first port gains in CNMI, initial Rota and Tinian assessments complete
items.description:
SANTA RITA, Guam — The Coast Guard Captain of the Port for Guam and the Marianas set a modified Port Heavy Weather Condition Whiskey for the Port of Tinian on Monday, reopening the port to daytime-only cargo operations and commercial traffic.

U.S. Coast Guard crews completed the first port surveys of the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, finishing initial assessments at Rota on Sunday and Tinian on Monday.
